questions.question: What is the primary purpose of conducting a job analysis?

options.text: To systematically identify the tasks, duties, and KSAOs required for a position

questions.explanation: Job analysis is a systematic process that identifies the tasks, duties, and responsibilities of a job, along with the Knowledge, Skills, Abilities, and Other characteristics (KSAOs) needed to perform it. It serves as the foundation for selection, training, and evaluation.

questions.question: Research shows that structured interviews are more effective than unstructured interviews because they:

options.text: Ask all candidates the same predetermined questions, allowing objective comparison
Are shorter and less expensive to conduct
Allow candidates to share personal stories freely

questions.explanation: Structured interviews ask all candidates the same predetermined questions, which allows for more objective comparison between applicants and is far more predictive of actual job performance than unstructured, conversational interviews.
